scope: B-owned ADC-DAC draft. Safe for isolated B mini runs via --cards-path; Terminal A should review before integration into docs/CONTRACT_REPAIR_CARDS.json.
description: Draft ADC-DAC repair cards selected from public contract failures and veriloga-skills references.

cards.title: Preserve ADC-DAC Artifact Boundary And Couple Code To Reconstruction
text: Preserve passed contracts: keep `vin`, `clk`, `rst_n`, `vout`, and `dout_3..0` observable; keep the input ramp and clock/reset behavior; do not rewrite the testbench when it is compile-clean.
Preserve the multi-file artifact boundary: keep `adc_ideal_4b.va` and `dac_ideal_4b.va`, keep module names `adc_ideal_4b` and `dac_ideal_4b`, and do not rename generated files to `_ref` names.

The Spectre testbench must include the exact emitted filenames: `ahdl_include "adc_ideal_4b.va"` and `ahdl_include "dac_ideal_4b.va"`. Do not include `adc_ideal_4b_ref.va` or `dac_ideal_4b_ref.va` unless you also emit those exact files, which this repair should not do.
If the current testbench already instantiates `adc_ideal_4b` and `dac_ideal_4b`, keep those instance module names and make only include filenames match the emitted files.
Use one held integer ADC code as the source of truth. On each rising `clk` after `rst_n` release, compute the clipped quantized code from `vin` and the public supply/reference range.
Drive `dout_3`, `dout_2`, `dout_1`, and `dout_0` from fixed bit positions of the held ADC code, with `dout_3` as MSB and `dout_0` as LSB.
In the DAC, decode the same scalar bit order into one integer code and drive `vout = vss + code/16.0 * (vdd-vss)`.
Do not tie `vout` directly to raw `vin` and do not leave `vout` constant; `vout` should be a quantized reconstruction coupled to the decoded code.
Keep `transition()` contributions unconditional and driven from held target variables.

cards.title: Use EVAS-Compatible Quantizer Math
text: When implementing quantizers in EVAS-compatible Verilog-A, use analog-expression numeric functions such as `floor(...)`, then explicitly store the result into an integer.
Avoid Verilog/SystemVerilog-style system-task math such as `$floor(...)` when EVAS feedback shows code coverage is stuck despite visible input activity.
Keep clipping explicit: if code is below zero, set it to zero; if code exceeds the maximum 4-bit value, set it to fifteen.
Declare temporary `real` and `integer` variables at module scope rather than relying on fragile block-local declarations inside event bodies.

proposals.rationale: adc_dac_ideal_4b_smoke has visible vin/clk/reset but unique_codes=1 and vout_span=0. The failed candidate is nearly correct except the ADC quantizer uses $floor(...), while a passing control uses EVAS-compatible floor(...). Prior repair also regressed the multi-file ADC/DAC artifact boundary.
Quantizer tasks can compile but remain behavior-stuck if unsupported numeric function forms are used.
